title: lactuca scariola var. integrata
intro: Lactuca scariola var. integrata, also known as wild lettuce or prickly lettuce, is a biennial plant in the dandelion family that has been used in traditional herbal medicine for centuries. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and sedative effects, which can be attributed to the presence of lactucarium, a glycoside with analgesic and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lactuca scariola var. integrata include relief from insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness, as well as pain relief and reduced inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
